creator: trix@basement.net
text:
Host has been swapped to use pkgNG by adding the following to /etc/make.conf:

# Use PKGNG since it's default in -CURRENT now (2012-10-13)
WITH_PKGNG=yes

The FreeBSD::Package package defined at /usr/local/bin/perl-after-upgrade line 129 is expecting a "+CONTENTS" file in a directory named for the package rather than the SQLite databases used by ports-mgmt/pkg

Versions used:

[trix@tara ~] 0 $ pkg info perl pkg
perl-5.16.2                    Practical Extraction and Report Language
pkg-1.0.1                      New generation package manager
[trix@tara ~] 0 $ 

Several p5-* packages are installed:

[trix@tara ~] 0 $ pkg info -x '^p5-.*' | wc -l
     190
[trix@tara ~] 0 $

Fix: 

I don't know enough about SQLite to address this issue.
How-To-Repeat: Install lang/perl followed by ports-mgmt/pkg.

Add "WITH_PKGNG=yes" line to /etc/make.conf

Convert package database with 'pkg2ng'.

Execute /usr/local/bin/perl-after-upgrade
[trix@tara ~] 0 $ time sudo perl-after-upgrade
\
---
Fixed 0 packages (0 files moved, 0 files modified)
Skipped 0 packages

real    0m0.260s
user    0m0.212s
sys     0m0.041s
[trix@tara ~] 0 $
